name: First Texas National Bank
description_short: Independent community bank headquartered in Floydada, Texas since 1890, offering checking, savings, loans, and mortgages with local decision-making.
description_long:
First Texas National Bank was established in 1890 and formally chartered in 1903 to serve Floyd County and surrounding Texas communities. For over 130 years, it has operated as an independent, locally owned institution with all major financial decisions made at their Floydada headquarters rather than in distant corporate offices. The bank is FDIC-insured and positions itself as a modern financial services provider that maintains traditional community banking values.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking products. Personal services include multiple checking account tiers (Regular Checking, Free Checking, NOW Account, Super NOW Account), savings accounts, money market accounts, vehicle loans, personal loans, secured loans, and credit cards. Business customers can access business checking, business NOW accounts, agricultural lending, commercial real estate loans, construction loans, equipment loans, operating lines of credit, letters of credit, and business credit cards. First Texas also provides mortgage services for home purchases and refinancing, positioning themselves as "Your Mortgage Experts."

What distinguishes First Texas is their emphasis on local ownership and community-focused decision-making. Their marketing explicitly contrasts their approach with larger institutions, promoting a "switch kit" to help customers transition from other banks and offering access to personal bankers. The bank emphasizes relationship-based banking and positions long-term community benefit as central to their business model. Their 1890 founding date and continuous operation in the same region for 130+ years provides historical credibility in West Texas.

First Texas National Bank is best suited for customers in the Floydada, Texas area and surrounding communities who value local decision-making and personal banking relationships. However, as a regional bank limited to their service territory, they may not be accessible to customers outside West Texas. The website provides limited detail on specific product terms, rates, or competitive advantages beyond their community-oriented positioning. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgage services for home purchase and refinancing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. Government
132+ years of continuous operation and local ownership since 1890
All loan decisions and service innovations made locally, not at corporate office
Comprehensive product suite including personal loans, business loans, mortgages, and credit cards
Personal banker access and relationship-focused banking approach
Specialized agricultural lending for regional business community
Online banking available for account access and management
cons: Limited geographic availability—appears to serve only Floydada and surrounding Floyd County area
Website provides no specific information on APRs, fee schedules, or rate competitiveness
No mention of digital-first features, mobile app functionality, or fintech integrations that larger banks offer
Limited transparency about mortgage products, terms, or refinancing options despite marketing them as experts
No information provided about checking account minimums, overdraft policies, or savings rates
best_for: Small business owners and farmers in Floyd County, Texas seeking agricultural and commercial lending
Floydada-area residents who value community banking relationships over national chains
Customers seeking local mortgage expertise for West Texas home purchases and refinancing
diagnosis: First Texas National Bank is best for customers in the Floydada, Texas area who prioritize local ownership and personal banker relationships over digital convenience or competitive rate shopping. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank appears to serve only West Texas communities and would not be accessible to customers outside their service territory.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
